summary: Excellent soundboard capture of a stellar park show featuring a soaring Dark Star → St Stephen → Eleven sequence and a fiery Lovelight.
review: The recording is praised as one of the better summer‑’69 soundboards, delivering crisp clarity and a clean mix (the Eleven track even includes its high‑green chilly winds intro). Musically the concert shines with a top‑drawer Dark Star into St Stephen into Eleven, plus an inspired Lovelight that some fans thought featured the Allman brothers. Overall the performance is celebrated as a standout moment in Atlanta’s music history.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers rate the show and recording highly (5‑star praise for both musical performance and soundboard quality), with only a single outlier focusing on Allman‑Brother confusion; weighted average yields a strong rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp, well‑balanced mix; noted as one of the better summer‑’69 soundboards. Eleven track includes its high‑green chilly winds intro; no major dropouts reported.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The set flows brilliantly, highlighted by a seamless Dark Star → St Stephen → Eleven suite and an exuberant Lovelight that energized the crowd.

band_member_comments.Keys: Tom Constanten’s organ work is singled out as a strong contribution, reinforcing why he was invited to join the band.
